error_signature: Header name must be a non-empty string
title: Empty header name in custom HTTP node
symptom: When executing a workflow with a custom HTTP node, n8n throws 'Header name must be a non-empty string'. This indicates that one of the headers defined in the node is missing a name.
applies_when: Headers are defined in the node configuration
not_this_if: Headers are correctly defined with non-empty names
root_cause: The error occurs because the custom HTTP node requires all headers to have valid names. If a header is defined without a name or with an empty string, n8n cannot process the request, leading to this error.
fix_steps: Open the custom HTTP node settings
Check the headers section for any empty header names
Provide valid names for all headers
Save the workflow and try executing it again
agent_instruction: Ensure all headers in the custom HTTP node have valid, non-empty names.
